# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS volumes for our application data. Focus on getting the instances running quickly. We'll add encryption and backup policies in phase 2 after we validate the architecture.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS data volumes (Phase 1: Quick Deployment)

Parameters:
  KeyName:
    Description: Name of an existing EC2 KeyPair to enable SSH access to the instance.
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    ConstraintDescription: Must be the name of an existing EC2 KeyPair.
  InstanceType:
    Description: EC2 instance type for the application server.
    Type: String
    Default: t3.micro
    AllowedValues:
      - t2.micro
      - t2.small
      - t3.micro
      - t3.small
      - t3.medium
      - m5.large
      - c5.large
    ConstraintDescription: Must be a valid EC2 instance type.
  AppVolumeSize:
    Description: Size of the application data EBS volume in GiB.
    Type: Number
    Default: 10
    MinValue: 1
    MaxValue: 16384 # Max for gp3 volume type

Mappings:
  AWSRegionToAMI:
    # Latest Amazon Linux 2023 AMI IDs (as of early 2024)
    # Please verify these AMIs are current or update to a preferred AMI.
    ap-east-1:
      AMI: ami-080c95029e2467d37
    ap-south-1:
      AMI: ami-0d046c87895e6f5d8
    ap-southeast-1:
      AMI: ami-0a2731c3855e4e138
    ap-southeast-2:
      AMI: ami-0466f9166f21221ac
    ap-northeast-1:
      AMI: ami-002d29469599d146c
    ap-northeast-2:
      AMI: ami-055a8286f0c69d80c
    ca-central-1:
      AMI: ami-0275529f52f30d061
    eu-central-1:
      AMI: ami-08e063c15370d0b7d
    eu-west-1:
      AMI: ami-0a6723b7b37bb941a
    eu-west-2:
      AMI: ami-05391c0ff2d1e9e2b
    eu-west-3:
      AMI: ami-0951a89c450c20f1c
    sa-east-1:
      AMI: ami-038c35a8f4c28f085
    us-east-1:
      AMI: ami-053b0d53c279acc90
    us-east-2:
      AMI: ami-0361f1c73eb2382e7
    us-west-1:
      AMI: ami-0544f8ae5047814b6
    us-west-2:
      AMI: ami-0fe9e6a035252b47e

Resources:
  EC2SecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Enable SSH access from anywhere
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-SSHAccess

  EC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      ImageId: !FindInMap
        - AWSRegionToAMI
        - !Ref AWS::Region
        - AMI
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      KeyName: !Ref KeyName
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !GetAtt EC2SecurityGroup.GroupId
      BlockDeviceMappings:
        # Root Volume - Default settings for most AMIs
        - DeviceName: /dev/xvda # Common root device name for Linux AMIs
          Ebs:
            VolumeSize: 8 # Default root volume size
            VolumeType: gp3
            DeleteOnTermination: true
        # Application Data Volume
        - DeviceName: /dev/xvdf # Common device name for additional EBS volumes
          Ebs:
            VolumeSize: !Ref AppVolumeSize
            VolumeType: gp3
            DeleteOnTermination: true # Volume will be deleted when the instance is terminated
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-AppInstance

Outputs:
  InstanceId:
    Description: The ID of the EC2 instance
    Value: !Ref EC2Instance
  PublicIp:
    Description: The Public IP address of the EC2 instance
    Value: !GetAtt EC2Instance.PublicIp
  SecurityGroupId:
    Description: The ID of the Security Group created for the instance
    Value: !GetAtt EC2SecurityGroup.GroupId
